items.q: Did Mussolini seize power by force in 1922?
items.correct: The March on Rome was a show of strength, but the king legally appointed Mussolini Prime Minister, so it was pressure rather than a violent coup.
items.misconception: Mussolini's Fascists fought their way into Rome and overthrew the king.
items.random_wrong: Mussolini won a landslide free election in 1922.
items.why: Students assume a violent takeover; the king's decision to appoint Mussolini made it constitutional, undercutting the Fascist myth of conquest.

items.q: Why did Italy invade Abyssinia in 1935?
items.correct: Mussolini sought empire, prestige and revenge for Adowa in 1896, defying the League of Nations to assert Italian power.
items.misconception: Italy invaded Abyssinia to stop a communist takeover.
items.why: Learners invent ideological motives; the war was about imperial glory and avenging the earlier 1896 humiliation at Adowa.

items.q: Was Italy a satisfied victor after the First World War?
items.correct: Despite being on the winning side, Italy gained less than promised, breeding the 'mutilated victory' resentment Fascists exploited.
items.misconception: Italy lost the First World War and was punished like Germany.
items.random_wrong: Italy stayed neutral throughout the First World War.
items.why: Students misremember Italy's side; it fought with the Allies from 1915 but felt cheated at the peace, fuelling later Fascism.

items.q: How did Mussolini become Prime Minister in 1922?
items.random_wrong: By a military coup against the king
items.why: It's Through the March on Rome. “By winning a free election” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What was the result of the Battle of Adowa in 1896?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Ethiopia defeated the Italian army. “Italy conquered Ethiopia” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Why did Italy feel cheated after the First World War?
items.correct: It received less territory than promised
items.why: The answer examiners want is: It received less territory than promised. “It lost all its colonies” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Abyssinia (Ethiopia). “Albania” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did the March on Rome take place?
items.why: The real figure is 1922 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
